query: How well does this ACT policy generalize across manipulated-object properties, and where is its first likely weakness?

proposal.hypothesis: A bounded appearance-only change to the target block, with geometry and collision unchanged, will cause ACT to miss strict hammer-block contact or official success, indicating that target appearance is a weakness independent of scale.

requested_perturbation.description: Generate one reviewed target-block appearance variant that changes only visual appearance while preserving the block mesh, pose, scale, collision geometry, and official success semantics.

task_need.description: TaskGen must generate or reuse a target-block appearance variant with appearance as the only changed factor and verify that geometry, collision, pose, scale, and success semantics are preserved.

tool_need.description: ToolGen must retrieve or generate an observable rule metric recording strict hammer-block physical contact, minimum functional-point XY error, and official success for the ACT rollout and matched expert probe.

proposal.rationale: Both tested scale directions failed to produce contact, but each has N=1 evidence and the smaller-scale run included visual false-positive diagnosis repairs. An appearance-only perturbation is the most informative supported next test because it separates a geometry/scale weakness from sensitivity to target visual properties while preserving the same task and policy.
